paper_title: Climate Simulations and Model Validation for Mid-to-late Holocene

methods.description: This method describes the setup of climate simulations covering several time slices of the mid-to-late Holocene using different models and resolutions. The purpose is to simulate past climate conditions and analyze the effects of astronomical and greenhouse gas forcings.

steps.description: First a transient continuous simulation is performed with the coupled atmosphere-ocean circulation model ECHO-G, composed by the ECHAM4 and the ocean model HOPE, at a spectral resolution of T30.
steps.searchable_summary: perform transient continuous simulation with ECHO-G model

steps.description: Select seven different time slices, at a temporal distance of approximately 1000 years from each other, from 6000 years ago down to the pre-industrial period, and conduct a simulation for each time slice with the atmosphere-only global circulation model ECHAM5 at a spectral resolution of T106.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct time slice simulations with ECHAM5 model

steps.description: Downscale ECHAM5 outputs with the regional climate model COSMO-CLM model version 4.8 clm 19 at a horizontal resolution of 0.44 longitude degrees, using 40 vertical levels.
steps.searchable_summary: downscale ECHAM5 outputs with COSMO-CLM model

methods.description: This method involves setting up the model to incorporate astronomical parameters and greenhouse gas concentrations for paleoclimate studies.

steps.description: Apply the routine of Prömmel et al. (2013) to estimate latitudinal and seasonal insolation at the top of the atmosphere based on Earth's astronomical parameters calculated by Berger (1978).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate insolation using Prömmel et al. routine

steps.description: Set the values of equivalent CO2 concentration, representing variations in CH4, CO2, and N2O, deduced from air trapped in ice cores.
steps.searchable_summary: set equivalent CO2 concentration from ice core data

methods.description: This method describes the validation and evaluation of the CCLM model for present-day climate conditions to ensure its accuracy and reliability for paleoclimate applications.

steps.description: Perform a control simulation with present values of orbital parameters and greenhouse gases to test the ability of the CCLM to reproduce present-day climate.
steps.searchable_summary: perform control simulation with present-day parameters

steps.description: Compare winter and summer seasonal means of temperature and precipitation from the CCLM simulations against the CRU and the E-Obs observational data sets.
steps.searchable_summary: compare seasonal means with observational data

steps.description: Validate temperature and precipitation values from the present-day control run through a Student's t test against the CRU and the E-Obs data sets.
steps.searchable_summary: validate control run with Student's t test
